basics.name: Hudson Mitchell-Pullman
basics.label: Visiting Scholar, Virginia Tech ASCEND³ Lab
basics.summary: I am a visiting scholar at Virginia Tech researching computer science education for AI-assisted programming. My broader interests lie at the intersection of HCI, learning science, and artificial intelligence. I've received over $20,000 in research grants from Emergent Ventures, Foundry10, and the Lift Off Grant to support my research. My interests lie in the use of intelligent tutoring systems and pedagogical AI tutors to support advanced problem solving, critical thinking, and end-user programming. I am mentored by Annapurna Vadaparty (UCSD), David Smith (Virginia Tech), and Qianou Ma (Carnegie Mellon).

work.name: Virginia Tech ASCEND³ Lab
work.summary: Taking a gap semester to conduct full-time research with Prof. David Smith on computer science education for AI-assisted programming.
highlights: Lead author on submissions to SIGCSE Virtual (already submitted), SIGCSE TS, Koli Calling, CHI, and ICER
Defining canonical computer science education learning objectives for AI-assisted programming
Evaluating a novel methodology for teaching requirement engineering with software testing
Postulating theories of the role of tacit and domain knowledge for AI-assisted programming
Conducting expert vs. novice requirement engineering assessments for the AI era
Conducting a class deployment study in a CS2 course at an R1 research institution

work.name: Papert Lab
work.summary: Founded Papert Lab, an applied learning science lab investigating the impact of AI on learning, memory, curiosity, and intelligent tutoring systems.
highlights: Funded by over $20,000 in research grants from education nonprofits and Emergent Ventures at George Mason University (Tyler Cowen)
See [papertlab.org](https://papertlab.org) for current work and design

work.name: UC San Diego CS Education Lab
work.summary: Mentored by Annapurna Vadaparty, a final-year Ph.D. student investigating problem decomposition and CS1 pedagogy.
highlights: Earned 2nd place at the Greater San Diego Science and Engineering Fair (GSDSEF)
Performed a deep 200-paper literature review on prompt engineering, HCI, and AI-assisted novice programming education
Drafted a research proposal analyzing the effect of manipulating input-output pairs in AI-assisted programming systems for pedagogy
Submitted to the Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED) conference with a novel requirement engineering pedagogical paradigm
Conducted a 100+ person comparison RCT on requirement engineering learning outcomes of high- vs. low-level testing

volunteer.summary: Founded a high-impact community of 20+ students interested in AI and computer science.
highlights: Mentored 10 high school students in prompt engineering, computer science, and human-AI interaction over six weeks
Coordinated a four-person team for the Presidential AI Challenge, building a real-time math tutor
Won two hackathons with members of the club
Presented to the World Language department on applications of AI in language acquisition

volunteer.summary: Youngest member of UCSD's invite-only AI startup and builders community.
highlights: Built 10+ projects and collaborated with UCSD students at hackathons
Attended weekly meetings with leading researchers and builders in computer and cognitive science

volunteer.summary: 12x award winner at international conferences; debated topics including the Middle East, geopolitics, antitrust, and economic policy.

education.studyType: Gap Semester — ASCEND³ Lab (Prof. David Smith)

highlights: Expected graduation 2028 from online high school

publications.name: Talk — Khan Academy AI in Education Summit
publications.summary: Presented on integrating Generative AI into Intelligent Tutoring Systems. Audience included Stanford students, the head of education at Anthropic, the director of the Stanford Accelerator for Learning, and other industry professionals, teachers, and students.

publications.name: Talk — Education Futures Podcast
publications.summary: Invited guest on an education-technology podcast alongside the leader of a successful French education think tank.

publications.name: Talk — Art of Problem Solving
publications.summary: Invited to speak with the CEO and technical staff on the role of conceptual math education in learning outcomes and AI-enhanced intelligent tutoring systems.
